name: Unlink success
description: Successful unlink of a loyalty program member from a partner. Mirrors the documented Response body for POST ('Unlink Program Member Partner' output representation) where message is null and status is true on success.

body.comments: Member requested to unlink the partner account.

notes:
METHOD DISCREPANCY: task said expected method=PUT, but the authoritative Developer Guide v67.0 explicitly documents HTTP methods=POST (lines 22506-22507) with 'Request body for POST' and 'Response body for POST'. Used the documented POST.

Path placeholders programName / membershipNumber / partnerName are positional path segments (doc resource: /loyalty/programs/programName/members/membershipNumber/partners/partnerName/unlink). Documented resource example uses v66.0 base: https://yourInstance.salesforce.com/services/data/v66.0/loyalty/programs/MarriottBonvoy/members/M0001/partners/AMEX/unlink. Available version = 66.0.

Request body (all OPTIONAL per property table, lines 22518-22531): comments (String), externalId (String, partner membership number of the member), linkNumber (String, unique linkage id), unlinkReasonType (String, reason to unlink). The doc's example value 'USER_REQUEST' for unlinkReasonType is the only documented sample; the full picklist of reason values is not enumerated in the guide (implementer-defined).

Response body 'Unlink Program Member Partner' (lines 33569-33591) has exactly two fields: message (String, nullable — null on success per JSON example) and status (Boolean — true/false). JSON example: {"message": null, "status": true}.

ERROR CASE: The doc states a Note (lines 22494-22495) that if membershipNumber or partnerName isn't provided the API returns an error requesting additional filter criteria, but NO error JSON body example is given in the guide. Therefore no error fixture was fabricated. An implementer adding a failure fixture should plausibly return {"message": "<error text>", "status": false} to match the documented schema (message=String, status=Boolean false), but the exact error message text is NOT documented.

Note the request INPUT schema is named 'Unlink Program Member Partner Input' (index at lines 24867, 27429); the RESPONSE schema is 'Unlink Program Member Partner' (definition at 33569).
